chunk_summary: This section provides full-day vegetarian regional menus for 12–23 month children, emphasizing low-cost substitutions and repeatable meal structures tailored to North/Central, South, and millet-heavy regions, with vegan adaptations and B12 supplementation guidelines.
learning_objectives: Understand how to structure full-day vegetarian menus for 12–23 month children across different Indian regions.
Learn low-cost substitutions for protein and energy sources in child feeding plans.
Recognize the importance of repeatable meal patterns and mandatory B12 supplementation for vegan families.
key_facts: Example North/Central breakfast: vegetable daliya with moong + ½ katori curd.
Example South dinner: ragi porridge with milk/curd or ragi dosa + dal side.
Low-cost protein substitutions: paneer replaced with soy nuggets/tofu; nuts replaced with roasted chana or peanut paste.
Vegan families must include reliable soy/legume anchors twice daily and mandatory vitamin B12 supplementation.
Millet-heavy options: bajra/jowar/ragi porridge or roti pieces + thick dal, with sesame or groundnut paste for energy.
reference_quote: Families should be able to run a similar day most days of the week.
